content_of_post_to_comment:
I'm not the most disciplined person. I've been trying to quit for about ten years.

I hit 127 days today, and this is what has worked for me.

1. AIM FOR THE PERSON YOU WANT TO BECOM. The best version of you, the focused one, the one with drive, the one God is proud of, already exists. However, that ideal you have is buried under layers of sin and other vices. You have so much more potential than you think. Find out who you are and how to reveal it to the world, and this will make it much easier for you to overcome sin and porn addiction specifically. You're not becoming someone. You're being revealed.

2. WILLPOWER ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH. Phone charges outside the bedroom. Use blockers if you're ok with that. Delete the social media apps and other time wasters if you don't need it for business. As many of us know, they are the gateway to a relapse. Also, take care of yourself. Every relapse I ever had was decided hours before it happened. I was engaging in fantasies, had been scrolling looking at "safe" pics on social media, etc. Take these as warning signs and immediately resolve the situation.

3. THE BATTLE IS WAGED IN THE MIND AND SPIRIT. All relapses begin first in the mind as thoughts. As thoughts they must be dismissed and destroyed and not allowed to inseminate the rest of your mind and world. During inevitable times of temptation, the lustful addictive thoughts will provide you with many reasons to relapse. At this time, if you do not present to them a more powerful army of reasons to NOT relapse, then you will lose that battle. Write down why you're doing this, and what's at stake if you remain addicted. Record yourself reading those out and listen to them during times of crisis, especially at night while trying to sleep. You can also use an app to do this which is what I prefer because the lines are generated for you and so won't get repetitive.

4. DO NOT JUST SIT THERE AND "FIGHT." Sitting there resisting is a negotiation, and you're negotiating with a professional demon who knows you intimately. The urge is a wave. It peaks and dies in 15-20 minutes IF you don't feed it. Do not have discussions with it, focus on what's at stake, and why you're doing this instead of trying to negotiate with it.

5. IF NOT NOW, WHEN? If you keep doing the same thing you've been doing, why would anything change? If you don't level yourself up, how will you level up your life? A magical moment is not going to come when it automatically and effortlessly comes together. One must take actions and break patterns. Do not leave it for tomorrow, do not put it off. You cannot afford to be addicted or to have your self-confidence as a man be chipped away daily by this addiction or any addiction. This is a matter of survival vs living a glorious and blessed life. Take it serious.

6. THE MIDDLE IS BRUTAL, EXPECT IT. After several weeks, the demon will begin to stir. At this point you must meet it with calmness, and if your reasons for doing this and your trust and purpose is set in stone with God, then you will destroy the enemy. However, expect symptoms such as a foggy mind, insomnia, restlessness, and the occasional "what's even the point." This is the crucible all of us must pass through. It's not unique. Follow the example of Christ who dismantled and trampled the serpent in the wilderness.

8. FILL THE HOLE PORN WILL LEAVE BEHIND. All that newfound time and energy has to go somewhere. Find your purpose. Find why you are born, and put effort into discovering what future you would need to be building for you to wake up excited to work on it or stay up late when the world is asleep just to work on your thing. Focus on improving yourself, for yourself, family, friends, and allow God to guide you towards becoming someone who will uplift mankind.

9. A SLIP IS A SLIP, IT IS NOT GAME OVER. Do not let the serpent whisper to you that "oh, since you messed up, no harm in doing it the entire day." That is complete bullshit. Do not ever give in to the serpent, or to those thoughts of doubt and reckless hedonism if you prefer a more non-religious way to put it. The point is to beat the devil. The point is to command yourself. Do not ever take direction or guidance from the enemy.

Keep going everyone, God bless.
comment: The point about relapses being decided hours before the actual moment hit home. A lot of people only fight the final urge, but the chain usually starts way earlier with small choices and thoughts. Learning to catch those early signals seems like a skill that matters far beyond quitting porn.
